feed.title: SAP Community - Data and Analytics
feed.subtitle: Data and Analytics Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: is there some customers using databricks for SuccessFactors data ?
entry.content: following SAP communication to partner with Databricks, i would be interessted to know if some customer have investigated some use cases to integrate SuccessFactors data ( past, cust, forecasted) into databricks.

entry.title: SAC USER ID
entry.content:
Hi All,

What is the best option for USER ID in SAC?

I am planning to use the employee ID as the USER ID, but users who were created directly in IAS do not have an employee ID. Which attributes from Azure and IAS would be best to use as the USER ID in SAC?

Is the login name field in IAS always provisioned as the USER ID in SAC via IPS?

Thank you so much.

Best Regards

entry.title: Sal Logon 8.00 AFO 2.6 Compatibility
entry.content:
Good day.

After updating sap logon from 7.6 to version 8.00. I can't see my connections to my S4HANA system from Analysis Office version 2.6

Has anyone had the same problem?

Thanks in advence.

entry.title: I am reaching out for guidance on the LMS Standard Item Status Story iReport, which I have been wor
entry.content: @gloriakurz  and whole team, I am reaching out for guidance on the LMS Standard Item Status Story iReport, which I have been working on recently. I imported the SFLMS Item Status version 1.0 from the Content Store, but the data appears to be inconsistent. As shown in the attached image, the item report has five pages, with the first page displaying "Due Soon" data. However, the calculation for "Items Assigned to Learners" shows 1092, which is derived from the major aggregate count of the dimension using different columns. I am struggling to understand the logic behind this calculation and would greatly appreciate any guidance or clarification on this matter. Unfortunately, there are no other reports available in the LMS system to cross-verify or validate the data. Please find the attached image for reference. I look forward to hearing from you and resolving this issue.

entry.title: SF_TIM_Employee_Timesheet_v2.0 report story
entry.content:
Hello, 

The SF_TIM_Employee_Timesheet_v2.0 report story documentation details that fields "Startdate" and "Enddate" are selected from the employee time sheet table. However, in all instances I implemented this template, the start date filter retrieves error and we cannot filter on it. 

In the screenshots we see clearly that the filter is functioning and that the fields are added to the timesheet detail tables, whereas when I import the story in the instance, the fields are not included in the tables. 

When previewing the query, these 2 fields do not appear in the columns view. 

When starting a new story, those 2 fields do not exist in the table. 

Investigating, I found this KBA : 3287193 - Start Time, End Time, and Start Date of a time recording in Time Sheet are returned empty in reports

But if I select the date from the employee time table it is either blank or an incorrect date, which do not represent the employee time sheet start date.

I have tried to change the format of the start date using this KBA: https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/mimes/call.htm?number=2986093 

but then it is a text field and I cannot use it to filter.

How can I add the start and end date of the employee time sheet table? How can we work with this story without errors in the dates? It is crucial for customers to be able to filter on dates as there are so many records in Time management. 

Any help is greatly appreciated

Thank you

entry.title: "Revolutionizing SAP Implementations: How AI is Changing the Game! 🚀"
entry.content:
Leveraging AI in SAP S/4HANA Implementations: A Game Changer?

Introduction

AI is rapidly transforming industries, but how does it fit into SAP S/4HANA implementations? While AI adoption in SAP environments is still low—only 5% of organizations currently use AI and ML—the potential is massive. With SAP Joule introducing 140 AI agents (and planning 400 by 2025), the focus is on improving processes and reducing inefficiencies.

Let's explore how AI can streamline requirement gathering, enhance blueprinting, and optimize the overall SAP implementation process.

 

The Current Landscape: AI in SAP Environments

Organizations still rely on traditional business processes, with SAP use cases focused on data warehousing, self-service analytics, and data discovery. However, integrating SAP data with non-SAP data remains a major challenge for achieving robust AI/ML models. Key barriers include:

  • Limited system access: Incomplete access to source and target systems creates bottlenecks.

  • Software usability issues: High complexity often leads to inefficiencies.

  • Data culture gaps: Organizations need to retrain and hire talent to harness AI’s full potential.

AI can bridge these gaps by enhancing usability and connectivity while reducing the dependency on deep technical expertise.

 

AI in SAP Implementation: Where It Fits

One of the most promising applications of AI in SAP S/4HANA projects is in the requirement gathering and blueprinting phase. Traditionally, this process is time-consuming and involves extensive coordination. AI-powered tools can revolutionize this by:

✅ Capturing and consolidating user requirements automatically
✅ Generating accurate documentation with minimal manual input
✅ Identifying process gaps for testing and training
✅ Reducing misalignment and inefficiencies in design and build phases

AI co-pilots and accelerators are already proving effective in recording user inputs, analyzing data, and structuring blueprints—allowing experts to validate and refine instead of starting from scratch.

 

How AI Streamlines SAP Implementation: A Step-by-Step Flow

1️⃣ AI-Powered Requirement Gathering:
AI captures existing processes and user pain points automatically, reducing reliance on meetings and manual inputs.

2️⃣ Intelligent Blueprinting:
AI-generated documents provide structured insights, identifying key requirements, business rules, and potential process optimizations.

3️⃣ Enhanced Testing & Training:
Gap documents generated by AI facilitate seamless testing, change management, and user training.

4️⃣ Optimized Implementation & Deployment:
With AI-driven documentation, project teams spend less time on redundant tasks, leading to faster go-lives and improved accuracy.

 

Interactive Discussion: AI in SAP Implementations

Q: How can AI-powered tools be used to capture, consolidate, and analyze user requirements and pain points before the design and build phases?

✅ AI accelerators can record user inputs on current processes and requirements, reducing the need for extensive coordination and meetings.
✅ AI consolidates these inputs and generates gap documents, essential for testing and training purposes.
✅ AI-generated documents are reviewed and validated by experts, leading to more accurate and efficient design solutions.
✅ Using AI in this way significantly reduces the time and effort required for requirement gathering and blueprinting, improving implementation quality.

 

Q: How can AI help address challenges in SAP implementations, such as incomplete access to source and target systems, software usability issues, and high complexity?

✅ AI improves ease of use and connectivity, reducing the need for technical skills.
✅ AI co-pilots help bridge the gap between user expectations and SAP reality.
✅ AI enhances connectivity to various source and target systems.
✅ AI improves the ability of technology to meet different use case requirements.

 

Future Outlook: The AI-Driven SAP Revolution

With AI’s role in SAP expanding, organizations that embrace AI-powered accelerators will gain a competitive edge in SAP S/4HANA transformations. While current AI tools focus on process improvement, the future lies in AI’s ability to automate and optimize the entire SAP implementation lifecycle.

💡 What’s Next?

  • More AI-driven automation in testing, training, and deployment

  • Improved integration of SAP and non-SAP data for better AI models

  • Expansion of AI agents in SAP Joule to enhance usability and decision-making

AI in SAP S/4HANA is no longer just an idea—it’s happening now. The question is: Are we ready to leverage it to its full potential?

entry.title: After running the script recorded in SAP, the system closes and errors apear
Can I display one field or another depending on filter values?
entry.content:
Hello, I'm new to SAC and still learning how it works and I,ve stumbled upon a headscratcher. 

I have a requirement where, in a table with multiple rows and columns, I'd like to display Field1 if the country filter value is set to Canada, and Field2 if the filter is set to US. All other fields in the table would be the same. 

The filter is an All Pages filter. 

I think this is feasable leveraging scripting, but I'm not sure how to go about it, any help would be appreciated. 

Thank you!

entry.title: CDS View for Compliance Check: Which SAP User holds which values from an authorization object
entry.content:
Hi,

Requirement for compliance checks:
Is there a Standard CDS for Compliance checks, at best, listening all Users, Roles , Auth.objects and their values ?

Problem:
-Got some x100 SAP Users.
-The SAP users have a multitude of roles ( of course ...;).
-For compliance checks  I want to formulate a CDS view bringing
back all SAP users and the values of  a certain Role.

Example:
Got in many roles the authorization object F_BKPF_BUK

Want to have a list
SAP_USER   | BUKRS_VALUE
---------------+-----------------------+
MEUSER1      511
MEUSER2      803
..

or even better:

SAP_USER   | BUKRS_VALUE | AUTH_OBJECT | ROLE
---------------+-----------------------+---------------------+--------------------+
MEUSER1    | 511                      | F_BKPF_BUK   | Z1_M1_D_510
MEUSER2    | 803                      | F_BKPF_BUK   | Z1_M1_D_803
...

Solutions:
Standard CDS available ?
Any else ?

Demarcation:
Its  NOT searched how to authorize a CDS via a
CDS Auth. Object, for ex: ...(Bukrs) = aspect pfcg_auth(F_BKPF_BUK, BUKRS).

Thank You !

Best Wishes !
Martin

entry.title: Available Material SAP 4/HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
I want to see the quantity of material available after any commitments. I can do it in the stock overview app but its by single material.

What I would like is a table showing the material, stock on hand, commitments and then what is available.   

If not in a view is there a CDS table that would show it? If so what is the CDS name.

entry.title: Benchmarking in SuccessFactors Story Report
entry.content:
We receive requirements from the business to compare/benchmark some HR metrics, for example, turnover. The idea is to allow managers look at the turnover rate of their scope, then see how that compares to the turnover level of the whole company/function.

Is this possible to achieve using Story Report without expanding the access permission of managers to see individual employee data outside of their own scope?

entry.title: analytics-cloud-export-api-wrapper: how to resolve an error at sac.getModelMetadata(model_id)?
entry.content:
KeyError                                  Traceback (most recent call last)
File ~\Documents\WinPython\WPy64-31241\notebooks\sacapi\sacapi.py:614, in SACConnection.getModelMetadata(self, providerID)
    613 mdMembers = {}
--> 614 for cdMember in currDimResponseJson["value"]:
    615     if "DATE" in cdMember:

KeyError: 'value'

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
Cell In[17], line 2
      1 # 4 Get metadata with connection from Data Import Service
----> 2 metadata = sac.getModelMetadata(model_id)
      3 dimension_keys = metadata.dimensions.keys()
      4 print([key for key in dimension_keys])

File ~\Documents\WinPython\WPy64-31241\notebooks\sacapi\sacapi.py:649, in SACConnection.getModelMetadata(self, providerID)
    647 except Exception as e:
    648     errorMsg = "Unknown error during token acquisition."
--> 649     if e.status_code:
    650         errorMsg = "%s  Status code %s from server.  %s" % (errorMsg, e.status_code, e.error)
    651         raise RESTError(errorMsg)

AttributeError: 'KeyError' object has no attribute 'status_code'

I would appreciate any help or pointers. Thanks! MJ

entry.title: Fiori OVP Analytical Card - Error OData Property 'NETPRICEAMOUNTAGGREGATE' already exists
entry.content:
Hi,

we have an OData V4 Service and I want to create an OVP with analytical cards. OData is V4 via RAP - No special analytical annotations. 

For my measure NETPRICEAMOUNT I will get the following error on opening the app in the error log:

Property 'NETPRICEAMOUNTAGGREGATE' already exists in entity type 'ET_6FF73BF54300DA439E9DDB6337A'

The aggregate property seems to be automatically generated. Somebody any Idea? 

Best regards Richard

entry.title: SAC to Datasphere
entry.content:
hi community,
Q1.) Can anyone guide me that how to create "Planning Model" in Datasphere ?

Q2.) Is this possible in SAC that a email notification generate after a condition meet ?

entry.title: Persistence of CDS view created on S4HANA into a separate HANA Database
entry.content:
Dear All,

Is there an option to persist CDS view data in the HANA database when HANA DB is being used as a middleware or data warehouse?

In my scenario, I have legacy data stored in HANA DB and OLTP data in S/4HANA. I would like to create a combined view of both datasets and expose it to SAC. My objective is to establish a mechanism that allows us to create CDS views in S/4HANA and transfer them to another HANA database, which will function as a middleware layer.

In my case, a custom SQL view (ZSQL) has been created in S/4HANA using CDS. How can this view be accessed or persisted in a separate HANA database that is acting as a middleware or data warehouse?
I have two HANA databases: one serving OLAP purposes (data warehouse/middleware) and the other being the S/4HANA OLTP system.

entry.title: Not able to copy data across models with shared dimension using Advanced Formula script(SAC)
entry.content:
Hi,

2 of my models has the same shared(public) dimensions and also different dimensions in it. I am trying to copy data between these 2 models(Measure based) in SAC using Advanced Formula with Link function. The validation is ok without errors but when i run its not copying the data. 

Also I tried using the LINK function in Advanced Formula with different dimensions with no shared(public dimension) in each model and i am able to copy the data between models.

Doesn't SAC supports copying data when there are shared dimension in models with LINK Function to copy the data across models.

Please let me know how to solve this.

entry.title: Non Standard Allocation in SAC
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are trying to create non-standard allocation logic via Advanced formula step. Any suggestions on How we can configure this via advanced formula or standard allocation or mix of both. 

Existing mapping between source and driver account data

ITEM CODEDiver account
SL1000AAC1
SL3000AAC2
SL8000AAC3
SL1000AAC4

Input data

AUDITTRAILVERSIONTIMECOMPCODEACCOUNTCOSTCENTERITEM_CODECOLLECTIONCHANNEL COUNTRY BRAND GENDERPRODUCT Amount
DT01BUDGET202412CC01AAC5COSTC1SL1000#2####200.00

Driver based on existing mix %

AUDIT TRAILVERSIONTIMECOMP CODEACCOUNTCOSTCENTERITEM_CODECOLLECTIONCHANNEL COUNTRY BRAND GENDERPRODUCT AmountExisting mix  Driver%
DT02BUDGET202412CC02AAC1SHOP1SL2000DM1M2USDM1M101,000.000.25
DT02BUDGET202412CC02AAC4SHOP1SL2000DM1M2USDM1M20500.000.125
DT02BUDGET202412CC02AAC1SHOP1SL2000DM1M2USDM1M302,500.000.625
DT02BUDGET202412CC02AAC2SHOP1SL2000DM1M2USDM1M403,500.001

Output after applying Driver to input Data

FINAL RESULT             
AUDIT TRAILVERSIONTIMECOMP CODEACCOUNTCOSTCENTERITEM_CODECOLLECTIONCHANNEL COUNTRY BRAND GENDERPRODUCT Amount
DT03BUDGET202412CC01AAC5COSTC1SL1000DM1M2USDM1M1050
DT03BUDGET202412CC01AAC5COSTC1SL1000DM1M2USDM1M2025
DT03BUDGET202412CC01AAC5COSTC1SL1000DM1M2USDM1M30125
             200.00

entry.title: SAP BDC Demo Environment
entry.content:
Hello All, 

Since the BDC trial version is now available, I’d like to create a demo using it. Could you please guide me on how to enable or build the content? Also, is it possible to showcase a demo using standard content, considering that the source systems are not properly maintained? I’m looking for any way to effectively pitch it to clients. I’m exploring options to work exclusively within BDC AI/ML part. The practice source systems are not properly maintained, making it impractical to establish connections with them. However, intelligent applications or insight apps require both source and target systems to be connected in order to enable the content. Are there any materials or documents available that can guide the implementation of Databricks AI/ML on raw data within an SAP BDC environment?

entry.title: Validation Check for Driver % Summing to 100% by Cost Center in SAC Table via Script
entry.content:
Hi All,

I’m working on a planning scenario in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and need help implementing a validation check on a table.

The table contains driver values that are expected to sum up to 100%, but the key requirement is that this summation must be validated with respect to each cost center, even when the table includes multiple combinations of other dimensions.

Currently, I’ve enabled the "Totals" row in the table, which allows for visual verification, but this doesn’t serve as an actual validation. What I’m looking for is:

A way to check programmatically or through scripting if the driver percentages sum to 100% for each cost center

If the sum is not 100% for any cost center, a popup message or alert should display, identifying which cost centers are affected

     Budget
     2024
     Driver
CostcenterCountryChannelBrandProduct 
CC1Totals   1
CC1#2##0.15
CC1#3##0.75
CC1#4##0.1

Is there any way to achieve this through scripting (e.g., in an analytic application) or through any other built-in SAC functionality?

Any guidance or examples would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Stack Analytical Models
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Recently the feature to stack Analytical models (AM) has been released.


I already see two benefits:

- Data Access Control on first AM (with options to add more detailed DAC on second AM...) 

- Calculations managed on the first AM

 

I was wondering if there are other benefits/ calculations possible due to this new feature?

Would love to know how others see this feature (filtering, restrictions,..) .

 

Kind regards
